title: Disengage [Action]
excerpt: The Disengage action lets a creature move without provoking opportunity attacks for the rest of the current turn.
markdown:
# Disengage [Action]

The Disengage action commits a creature's [[action]] to careful withdrawal. For the rest of the current turn, that creature's movement does not trigger [[opportunity_attack|opportunity attacks]].

## Core Effect

- Disengage changes how movement interacts with hostile reach during the current turn
- the creature still spends its own movement normally after taking the action
- the protection applies only for the rest of that turn; it does not continue into later turns or rounds

## Movement Context

A creature can Disengage before leaving melee, repositioning through a threatened area, or retreating to safer ground. The action does not create extra movement, so the creature is still limited by its [[speed]] and any current movement costs.

## Limits

- Disengage does not block every kind of [[reaction]] or triggered effect
- Disengage does not end grapples, restraints, or other states that prevent movement in the first place
- Disengage only changes the specific trigger that would normally allow an opportunity attack
